# data.data { #spotforecast2_safe.data.data }

`data.data`

Data structures for input and processed data.

## Classes

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Data](#spotforecast2_safe.data.data.Data) | Container for input time series data. |
| [Period](#spotforecast2_safe.data.data.Period) | Class abstraction for the information required to encode a period using RBF. |

### Data { #spotforecast2_safe.data.data.Data }

```python
data.data.Data(data)
```

Container for input time series data.

#### Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

| Name   | Type                                           | Description                                             |
|--------|------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| data   | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`) | pandas DataFrame containing the input time series data. |

#### Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [from_csv](#spotforecast2_safe.data.data.Data.from_csv) | Load data from a CSV file. |
| [from_dataframe](#spotforecast2_safe.data.data.Data.from_dataframe) | Create a new Data instance from an existing DataFrame. |

##### from_csv { #spotforecast2_safe.data.data.Data.from_csv }

```python
data.data.Data.from_csv(
    csv_path,
    timezone,
    columns=None,
    parse_dates=True,
    index_col=0,
    **kwargs,
)
```

Load data from a CSV file.

The CSV must contain a datetime column that becomes the DataFrame index.
The index is localized to the provided timezone if it is naive, and then
converted to UTC.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name        | Type                                                                   | Description                                                                                                                                              | Default    |
|-------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| csv_path    | [Path](`pathlib.Path`)                                                 | Path to the CSV file.                                                                                                                                    | _required_ |
| timezone    | [Optional](`typing.Optional`)\[[str](`str`)\]                          | Timezone to assign if the index has no timezone. Must be provided if the index is naive.                                                                 | _required_ |
| columns     | [Optional](`typing.Optional`)\[[List](`typing.List`)\[[str](`str`)\]\] | List of column names to include. If provided, only these columns will be loaded from the CSV (optimizes reading speed). If None, all columns are loaded. | `None`     |
| parse_dates | [bool](`bool`) or [list](`list`)                                       | Passed to ``pd.read_csv``. Defaults to True.                                                                                                             | `True`     |
| index_col   | [int](`int`) or [str](`str`)                                           | Column to use as index. Defaults to 0.                                                                                                                   | `0`        |
| **kwargs    | [Any](`Any`)                                                           | Additional keyword arguments forwarded to ``pd.read_csv``.                                                                                               | `{}`       |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                        | Description                               |
|--------|---------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|
| Data   | [Data](`spotforecast2_safe.data.data.Data`) | Instance containing the loaded DataFrame. |

###### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type                       | Description                                                 |
|--------|----------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [ValueError](`ValueError`) | If the CSV does not yield a DatetimeIndex.                  |
|        | [ValueError](`ValueError`) | If the index is timezone-naive and no timezone is provided. |

###### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> from spotforecast2_safe.data import Data
>>> data = Data.from_csv(
...     Path("data.csv"),
...     timezone="UTC",
...     columns=["target_col"]
... )
```

##### from_dataframe { #spotforecast2_safe.data.data.Data.from_dataframe }

```python
data.data.Data.from_dataframe(df, timezone, columns=None)
```

Create a new Data instance from an existing DataFrame.

The DataFrame must have a datetime index. The index is localized to the
provided timezone if it is naive, and then converted to UTC.

###### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name     | Type                                                                   | Description                                                                                                                          | Default    |
|----------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| df       | [pd](`pandas`).[DataFrame](`pandas.DataFrame`)                         | Input DataFrame containing data.                                                                                                     | _required_ |
| timezone | [Optional](`typing.Optional`)\[[str](`str`)\]                          | Timezone to assign if the index is naive. Must be provided if the index has no timezone.                                             | _required_ |
| columns  | [Optional](`typing.Optional`)\[[List](`typing.List`)\[[str](`str`)\]\] | List of column names to include. If provided, only these columns will be selected from the DataFrame. If None, all columns are used. | `None`     |

###### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                        | Description                                 |
|--------|---------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Data   | [Data](`spotforecast2_safe.data.data.Data`) | Instance containing the provided DataFrame. |

###### Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type                       | Description                                                 |
|--------|----------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [ValueError](`ValueError`) | If the DataFrame index is not a DatetimeIndex.              |
|        | [ValueError](`ValueError`) | If the index is timezone-naive and no timezone is provided. |

### Period { #spotforecast2_safe.data.data.Period }

```python
data.data.Period(name, n_periods, column, input_range)
```

Class abstraction for the information required to encode a period using RBF.

#### Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

| Name        | Type                                                  | Description                                                            |
|-------------|-------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| name        | [str](`str`)                                          | Name of the period (e.g., 'hour', 'day').                              |
| n_periods   | [int](`int`)                                          | Number of periods to encode (e.g., 24 for hours).                      |
| column      | [str](`str`)                                          | Name of the column in the DataFrame containing the period information. |
| input_range | [Tuple](`typing.Tuple`)\[[int](`int`), [int](`int`)\] | Tuple of (min, max) values for the period (e.g., (0, 23) for hours).   |

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> from spotforecast2_safe.data import Period
>>> period = Period(name="hour", n_periods=24, column="hour", input_range=(0, 23))
>>> period.name
'hour'
```
